data.title: Good News for Tesla! Canada Reopens Door to China-Made EVs
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) could be well positioned to benefit after Canada announced a new trade deal on Friday that sharply lowers tariffs on electric vehicles made in China. The move reopens a market that had largely been shut since 2024, allowing a limited number of China-built EVs back into Canada at lower tariff rates.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Under the deal, Canada will permit up to 49,000 vehicles a year to be imported from China at a 6.1% tariff, down from 100%. While access remains capped, the quota could rise over time, creating new opportunities for automakers with existing production and sales networks.

Why Tesla Is Better Positioned Than Rivals

Tesla is well placed to benefit because it prepared early. In 2023, the company upgraded its Shanghai factory to build Canada-specific versions of the Model Y and began shipping them to Canada. Those exports stopped in 2024 when tariffs jumped, forcing Tesla to rely on vehicles from the U.S. and Europe instead.

With the new rules in place, analysts said Tesla could restart China-to-Canada shipments faster than others. Its Shanghai plant is Tesla's largest and lowest-cost factory, and many lower-priced models, such as parts of the Model 3 lineup, are mainly built there.

Moreover, Tesla has a strong sales setup in Canada, with dozens of stores already in place. That gives it a clear advantage over Chinese rivals like BYD (BYDDF) and Nio (NIO), which do not yet have a direct retail presence in the country.

What Could Limit Tesla's Upside?

The deal does come with restrictions. Half of the import quota is reserved for vehicles priced under 35,000 Canadian dollars, a level below most Tesla models. Even so, experts believe that Tesla's lineup and flexible production give it room to adjust faster than competitors.

While Chinese automakers are also likely to benefit from the new rules, Tesla's early preparation, low-cost Shanghai factory, and established sales network in Canada leave it well placed as the market reopens.

Are Tesla Shares a Good Buy? 

According to TipRanks, TSLA stock has received a Hold consensus rating, with 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the last three months. The average Tesla stock price target is $397.47, suggesting a potential downside of 9.15% from the current level.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Apple Regains China Crown as iPhone Shipments Jump 28%
data.description:
Apple Inc. (AAPL) has reclaimed the top spot in China's smartphone market after iPhone shipments surged 28% during the key holiday quarter, according to Counterpoint Research. The rebound came even as the wider market slipped, showing that demand for premium phones remains strong.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Counterpoint said Apple's latest iPhones accounted for about one in five smartphones shipped in China during the December quarter. The gains came largely at the expense of Huawei Technologies and Xiaomi, both of which saw double-digit declines. Overall smartphone shipments in China fell 1.6%, making Apple's performance stand out.

High-End Phones Hold Firm despite Chip Shortage

Apple's gains came at a time when the industry is dealing with a shortage of memory chips. Many tech firms have warned that tight supply and rising prices could hit phone makers, especially at the low end. The issue has grown as chipmakers focus more on advanced memory used in AI systems for companies like Nvidia (NVDA).

Counterpoint said premium phones have been less affected by the shortage. That has helped Apple, which sells mostly high-end devices. Analysts expect many brands to cut back on cheaper models to protect margins as costs rise.

Where Apple Stands Now in China's Premium Market

For the full year, Apple finished just behind Huawei in China, with both holding about 17% market share. Apple's shipments rose 7.5% in 2025, helped by steady demand for its main iPhone models. However, the iPhone Air was a weak spot, as its late launch in China failed to gain much interest.

Even so, the strong holiday-quarter performance shows Apple's brand remains resilient in China, despite ongoing pressure across the wider smartphone market.

Is Apple a Buy or Sell Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on AAPL stock based on 19 Buys, 11 Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average AAPL price target of $299.69 per share implies 17.28% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: The Stock Market Is Closed on MLK Day, but Here's What Goldman Sachs Says to Buy When Trading Resumes
data.description:
The stock market isn't open on Martin Luther King Jr. Day, but that doesn't mean investors are sitting idle. While markets are closed in observance of the holiday, Wall Street's focus is already shifting toward what could be top trades once trading resumes.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

One name drawing attention from Goldman Sachs analyst Asad Haider is BioNTech (NASDAQ:BNTX). On Friday, Haider upgraded the stock from Neutral to Buy and lifted his 12-month price target to $142 from $115, implying 30% upside from current levels.

Haider believes BioNTech sits "at the nexus of significant developments occurring in the oncology field," pointing to the company's expanding footprint across next-generation immuno-oncology and antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs). In Haider's view, that positioning could give BioNTech a meaningful role in what the analyst describes as the "next wave" of cancer treatment innovation – well beyond the company's legacy COVID-19 vaccine business.

Importantly, the bullish call isn't built around a single asset or trial. Haider highlights BioNTech's broad, strategic portfolio, which includes novel PD-1/VEGF bispecifics, ADC programs, and mRNA-based cancer vaccines. The analyst argues this diversified approach allows the company to pursue multiple shots on goal across large oncology indications, while also reducing dependence on any single readout.

While COVID-related revenue continues to generate meaningful cash flow, Haider believes the oncology pipeline is set to become the more material driver of stock performance. The analyst notes that BioNTech shares have lagged in 2025, in part due to lingering overhang from the COVID franchise. However, he sees the narrative beginning to shift as the company moves deeper into late-stage development, with a series of clinical milestones expected to build into 2026.

In fact, Haider points to 2026 as the start of a multi-year catalyst-rich period, with BioNTech expecting Phase 2 and Phase 3 data readouts across its oncology programs. In his framework, success does not need to be binary; progress in individual indications could independently unlock value and steadily rebuild investor confidence.

Haider argues that BioNTech's current share price effectively values the company close to its cash and COVID business alone, assigning limited credit to its oncology assets. In his view, that creates what he describes as "asymmetric upside," particularly if upcoming data begin to validate the pipeline's breadth and potential.

"We believe BNTX has strategically positioned itself to be a leader in this next wave of potential advancements, presenting a unique risk/reward opportunity," the analyst summed up.

Overall, BNTX has picked up 15 recent analyst reviews, and these break down to 12 Buys and 3 Holds to support the Strong Buy consensus rating. The shares are priced at $109 and have an average target price of $141.93, which is practically the same as Haider's. (See BNTX stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Down on Trump's Greenland-Linked Tariff Threat
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell late Sunday night after President Donald Trump warned of new trade tariffs on several European countries. The move is tied to negotiations over Greenland. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were down 1.13%, 0.73%, and 0.86%, respectively, at 12:58 a.m. EDT on January 19.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

U.S. markets will be closed tomorrow in observance of Martin Luther King Jr. Day.

Over the weekend, Trump said the U.S. would impose an extra 10% tariff on goods from Denmark, Norway, Sweden, France, Germany, the Netherlands, Finland, and the UK starting February 1. If no deal on Greenland is reached, the tariff would rise to 25% by June.

European officials are also weighing possible retaliatory measures, raising concerns that the dispute could escalate into a broader trade standoff.

U.S. stocks ended last week mostly flat. Gains in semiconductor stocks were offset by weakness in bank shares and ongoing policy concerns. The Dow fell 0.17% to 49,359, the S&P 500 slipped 0.06% to 6,940, and the Nasdaq edged down 0.07% to 25,529.

Earnings will also be in focus this week, with several companies reporting results, including Netflix (NFLX), 3M (MMM), United Airlines (UAL), Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), and Intel (INTC).

data.title: Here's What Could Push ARM Stock Higher or Lower in 2026
data.description:
So far in 2026, Arm Holdings (ARM) has been drawing attention from investors and analysts for both exciting strategic moves and growing concerns about its fundamentals. The company is best known for designing the processor architecture used in most smartphones, and it earns money through licensing and royalties rather than making chips itself.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

However, Arm recently reorganized its business to pursue new growth opportunities, while some Wall Street firms are worried about how sustainable the company's growth path really is. With shares trading well below recent highs and some mixed analyst sentiment, there are clear potential catalysts that could boost the stock, as well as risks that might push it lower.

Catalysts that May Push the Stock Higher

One of the most important recent developments for Arm is its decision to launch a new Physical AI unit. Announced at this year's Consumer Electronics Show, this move reorganizes Arm's business into three main areas:

  1. Cloud and AI
  2. Edge
  3. Physical AI

By doing this, Arm is growing beyond its core smartphone and PC markets and positioning itself more directly in robotics and intelligent machines. Over time, management believes that these areas could become meaningful new sources of growth.

At the same time, this expansion into robotics and physical AI could help Arm reduce its reliance on slower-growing mobile markets. As robots, autonomous systems, and AI-driven machines become more common, demand for Arm's low-power, energy-efficient designs could increase. If this happens, Arm could earn more licensing and royalty revenue without needing to manufacture chips itself, which fits well with its existing business model.

Risks to Watch Out For

However, these opportunities come with real risks that investors should not ignore. Most notably, five-star Bank of America analyst Vivek Arya recently downgraded Arm from Buy to Hold because he believes that the company's current valuation does not fully reflect its underlying risks. Indeed, a key concern is that Arm's reported growth has been heavily influenced by SoftBank (SFTBY), its controlling shareholder. Once those revenues are adjusted for, Arya worries that Arm's true sales growth may look less impressive.

At the same time, Arm's share price has been on a downtrend over the past several months, which means that investor sentiment is currently negative and can lead to further downward momentum if short-sellers pile in. This decline is likely due to a mix of factors, including slowing smartphone demand and uncertainty over how quickly newer initiatives like robotics can turn into real profits.

Finally, competition is a constant challenge. Indeed, the AI and chip-design space is becoming more crowded, with rival architectures and alternative licensing models gaining attention. If Arm fails to innovate quickly enough or struggles to convert its new Physical AI strategy into meaningful customer adoption, its long-term market position and valuation could come under more pressure.

Is ARM Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on ARM stock based on 14 Buys, five Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average ARM price target of $170.59 per share implies 61.3% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Why Wall Street Is Turning More Confident on Alibaba's AI Push
data.description:
Alibaba Group Holding (BABA) is gaining support from Wall Street as analysts grow more confident that AI could power the company's next leg of growth. Recent updates around Alibaba's Qwen app and its cloud unit show the company moving beyond simple chat tools toward AI that can handle real tasks, which analysts view as a clear advantage.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Jefferies: Alibaba Is Ready for AI Agents

Jefferies analyst Thomas Chong reaffirmed a Buy rating and a $225 price target, saying Alibaba is well placed for the rise of AI agents. The 5-star analyst pointed to the latest Qwen app update, which now works closely with Alibaba's main services such as Taobao, Alipay, Fliggy, and Amap. This lets users shop, order food, plan trips, and make payments in one place.

Chong said this shows how Alibaba is turning AI into a tool that completes tasks, not just answers questions. He also highlighted strong momentum at Alibaba Cloud, noting that many business customers already use both AI models and computing services. With most companies still early in AI adoption, Jefferies sees a long runway for cloud growth ahead.

Morgan Stanley: Consumer AI Is Picking Up

Morgan Stanley analyst Gary Yu also stayed positive, keeping an Overweight rating and a $180 price target. He said consumer AI and task-based tools will likely be a major focus this year. Yu noted that the Qwen app can now handle over 400 daily tasks and has crossed 100 million monthly users in just two months.

He believes Alibaba's wide range of services gives Qwen a solid chance to become an all-in-one AI app. Morgan Stanley expects further gains in user activity and steady cloud growth to support the stock, even though higher spending to attract users could weigh on profits in the near term.

Is Alibaba Stock a Buy Now? 

Wall Street remains constructive on the stock. Alibaba stock carries a Strong Buy consensus rating, based on 13 Buy ratings and one Hold over the past three months. The average BABA price target stands at $203.66, suggesting roughly 19.15% upside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: 10-Year Treasury Yield Leaps to 4-Month High as Trump Dials Down Stance on Iran
data.description:
The 10-year Treasury yield is up by 6.7 bps to a four-month high of 4.227% as investors digest easing geopolitical tensions and President Trump's nominee to replace Fed Chair Jerome Powell.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"I greatly respect the fact that all scheduled hangings, which were to take place yesterday (Over 800 of them), have been cancelled by the leadership of Iran," Trump said in a Truth Social post on Friday. Treasuries are viewed as a safe-haven asset, with easing geopolitical risks reducing demand for notes and bonds and pushing yields higher. The yield on Treasuries moves in the opposite direction of their prices.

Why is the 10-Year Treasury Yield Up Today?

Trump also noted that he would like White House National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett to remain in his current position. Following the comment, the odds of Trump nominating Hassett as Fed Chair dived to 17% from 31%, while former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh's odds rose to 61% from 34%.

Warsh is viewed by some economists as less likely to cut rates than Hassett. In other words, the 10-year Treasury yield rose as investors priced in a more hawkish Fed outlook under a possible Warsh nomination.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Nvidia Stock (NVDA) Forecast: Bear Case vs. Bull Case Scenario for 2026
data.description:
American chip giant Nvidia's stock (NVDA) continues to dominate headlines as the powerhouse behind the AI revolution, with its data center GPUs (graphics processing units) fueling record growth. After a robust 40% growth in 2025, investors face a pivotal question: will Nvidia sustain its momentum amid competition and macroeconomic headwinds, or surge to new heights on robust AI adoption? This article pits the bear case, steady but moderated gains, against the bull case of breakthrough innovations and market share expansion.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On TipRanks, the lowest price target of $200 suggests 6.9% upside potential over the next twelve months. Meanwhile, the highest price target of $352 implies 88.2% upside potential. Here's a closer look at both analysts' views.

NVDA Bear Case – $200 Price Target

Phillip Securities' four-star analyst Yik Ban Chong has a Buy rating on NVDA with a price target of $200. Following Nvidia's Q3 FY26 results, Chong raised his full year fiscal 2026 revenue forecast by 4% and profit by 10%. He attributed the faster ramp-up of the Blackwell GB300 chips, which would also boost gross margins by 0.5 percentage points.

While Chong remains concerned about the circular nature of AI deals with OpenAI ($100 billion) and Anthropic ($10 billion), he believes they represent less than 10% of Nvidia's $380 billion revenue visibility through the end of 2026.

He sees strong, persistent organic demand from hyperscalers' increased capex and sovereign deals in South Korea, Europe, and the UAE. Chong noted that NVDA traded at a forward P/E of 44.8x, below its historical 52.2x average.

NVDA Bull Case – $352 Price Target

Evercore ISI's five-star analyst Mark Lipacis also rates NVDA a Buy, with a $352 price target. He praised Nvidia's strong Q3 FY26 earnings, which showed faster revenue growth and more product availability. He also pointed to better supply of Blackwell-based GB200 and GB300 AI platforms, with Blackwell compute revenue jumping 48% quarter-over-quarter to add over $13 billion.

Nvidia reported $500 billion in data-center orders for Blackwell and advanced Rubin platforms through 2025-26. Despite tight supply concerns, Lipacis highlighted Nvidia's 32% higher Q3 inventory and 63% jump in supply commitments to handle these orders.

He views the projected $493 billion revenue for 2025-26 as conservative amid strong trends, signaling big stock upside if demand holds. His $352 price target is based on a 35x multiple on 2030 base-case EPS of $14.74, discounted back to 2026.

Is Nvidia a Good Buy Right Now?

Wall Street remains highly optimistic about Nvidia's long-term outlook. On TipRanks, NVDA commands a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell rating. The average Nvidia price target of $263.44 implies 40.8% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: UAA, MARA, HIMS: The 10 Most Shorted Stocks in the Market
data.description:
It's a new year and the stock market is off to a strong start. But that doesn't mean there aren't bears lurking on Wall Street.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The S&P 500 index is off to a decent start in 2026, rising more than 1% through the first two weeks of January. Ryan Detrick, Chief Market Strategist at wealth advisory firm Carson Group, points out that when the S&P 500 is up 1% or more after the first five trading days, then the full year is positive 87% of the time, with an average gain of 16%.

However, despite the positive start to the year, there are still plenty of stocks that investors and traders are shorting, meaning that they're betting on the share price declining. And the 10 most shorted stocks that have attracted bearish bets on Wall Street include the following names…

Wall Street's 10 Most Shorted Stocks

  1. Under Armour (UAA) – 34.96% short interest
  2. Recursion Pharmaceuticals (RXRX) – 32.23%
  3. CleanSpark (CLSK) – 31.55%
  4. Solaris Energy Infrastructure (SEI) – 30.88%
  5. Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) – 30.60%
  6. SoundHound AI (SOUN) – 28.59%
  7. MARA Holdings (MARA) – 27.45%
  8. Upstart Holdings (UPST) – 27.42%
  9. Applied Digital (APLD) – 27.27%
  10. Kohl's (KSS) – 25.86%

The most heavily shorted stocks range from struggling retailers such as Under Armour and Kohl's, to cryptocurrency mining firm MARA Holdings and telehealth concern Hims & Hers Health. All of the stocks on the list have experienced volatility in recent months.

As for the least shorted stocks on Wall Street currently, they are e-commerce giant Amazon (AMZN) and discount retailer Walmart (WMT). Less than 1% of AMZN and WMT stock is currently sold short, market data shows.

Is UAA Stock a Buy?

The stock of Under Armour has a consensus Hold rating among 18 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on four Buy, 11 Hold, and three Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average UAA price target of $6.00 implies 3.81% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: 'Ryanair CEO Is an Utter Idiot,' Says Elon Musk in Latest Public Feud
data.description:
Elon Musk escalated a public feud on Friday by calling Ryanair (RYAAY) CEO Michael O'Leary an "utter idiot" and saying that he should be fired. The clash began earlier in the week after O'Leary ruled out installing Musk's Starlink internet service on Ryanair's aircraft. According to O'Leary, adding Starlink antennas would increase drag, raise fuel costs, and cost the airline as much as $250 million per year. As a result, he said Ryanair had no interest in adopting the technology.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Musk quickly pushed back on his social media platform X by arguing that O'Leary was "misinformed" and that the airline did not properly understand how to measure the fuel impact of Starlink equipment. However, the dispute became worse when O'Leary later spoke to Irish radio station Newstalk. During the interview, he said that Musk knew "zero" about aviation and aerodynamic drag. In addition, he openly called the billionaire an "idiot" and criticized X as a "cesspit." Unsurprisingly, the exchange did not end there.

Musk, whose space company SpaceX operates Starlink, responded again on Friday by posting, "Ryanair CEO is an utter idiot. Fire him." When one user suggested Musk should simply buy Ryanair and remove O'Leary himself, Musk replied, "Good idea." The argument comes as airlines become a growing market for Starlink, which uses thousands of low-Earth-orbit satellites to deliver faster in-flight WiFi. In fact, more than two dozen airlines are already rolling out the service, though analysts say its premium nature makes it more attractive to long-haul and full-service carriers rather than budget airlines like Ryanair.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

When it comes to Elon Musk's companies, most of them are privately held. However, retail investors can invest in his most popular company, Tesla (TSLA). Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and eight Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $394.12 per share implies 10% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri
